title: Maybe a bug regarding the labels of the symbol table
body:
Hello, 

I am trying to use yosys to generate an AIGER circuit from a Verilog file. I especially need the generated AIGER symbol table to use the names specified in the Verilog file for the inputs, outputs etc. 
For that, I tried using the following command: `yosys -p "synth; abc -script abc.script" input_file.v` with the abc.script containing several commands and ending with `write_aiger -s out.aig`.
However, I observed that the symbol table generated like that did not use the names used in the Verilog input file but rather some possibly default values like `n0, n1`. 
In order to debug this, I tried running the commands seperately by first calling `yosys` and there running first `read_verilog input_file.v`, then `synth` and then `write_blif test.blif` (in order to be able to switch to yosys-abc). Then I `exit` yosys and start `yosys-abc`, where I first executed `read_blif test.bilf` before running the commands contained in `abc.script`, the last one being `write_aiger -s out.aig`. To my surprise, I observed that the symbol table now contained the desired labels. 
So I added a `write_blif` and a `read_blif` step to my command resulting in `yosys -p "synth; write_blif test.blif; abc -script abc_2.script" input_file.v` where the abc_2.script is the same as the abc.script but with a `read_blif test.blif` command added to the beginning of the file. Running the command like this again resulted in the desired output regarding the symbol table. 

I am now wondering why the intermediate writing and reading of the BLIF file makes a difference at all? I would have expected, that all necessary information is also passed internally from yosys to yosys-abc directly and that there would be no need to write a temporary BLIF file. However, exactly this seems to make a difference, so maybe there is a bug or I am overlooking something?

I am really looking forward to better understanding this issue and thank you in advance for all explanations offered. Please let me know if you need more information in order to be able to reproduce this issue. 

Best regards, 
Norine
